word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɡˈʊd fˈɛɹ fˈeɪs nˈiːdz nˈoʊ pˈeɪnt

senses.definition: used to imply that a person's true beauty lies in their virtues, personality, and inner qualities, which surpass the need for external enhancements

examples.x: I learned that confidence and self-love are more important than any beauty product, because a good face needs no paint.

word_list.word: a fair face may hide a foul heart
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ fˈɛɹ fˈeɪs mˈeɪ hˈaɪd ɐ fˈaʊl hˈɑːɹt

senses.definition: used to warn that appearances can be deceiving, so it is important to look beyond a person's outward appearance or charm to understand their true character or intentions

examples.x: It's important to remember that a fair face may hide a foul heart, and to take the time to get to know someone before making judgments about them.

word_list.word: a monkey in silk is a monkey no less
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ mˈʌnki ɪn sˈɪlk ɪz ɐ mˈʌnki nˈoʊ lˈɛs

senses.definition: used to say that external appearances or material possessions do not change one's true nature or inherent qualities

examples.x: I try to judge people based on their actions, not their outward appearance, because a monkey in silk is a monkey no less.

word_list.word: every couple is not a pair

senses.definition: used to suggest that people's lives and relationships may appear better or more ideal from a distance, but the reality may be different when seen up close
used to imply the importance of a rich inner life for true satisfaction, as external success cannot guarantee inner fulfillment, and even the successful may feel empty or unhappy
used to suggest that those who make the most noise or attract the most attention may not have much depth or substance to their words or actions

examples.x: Social media can be a platform for anyone to voice their opinions, but often shallow streams make most din as people compete for attention with flashy headlines and clickbait.

senses.definition: used to imply that people or things that appear calm or unremarkable on the surface may actually have great depth or complexity

word_list.word: you can put lipstick on a pig, but it is still a pig
word_list.pronunciation: juː kæn pˌʊt lˈɪpstɪk ˌɑːn ɐ pˈɪɡ bˌʌt ɪt ɪz stˈɪl ɐ pˈɪɡ

senses.definition: used to convey the idea that no matter how much one tries to disguise or enhance the appearance of something or someone, their true nature or essence remains unchanged

word_list.word: all are not thieves that dogs bark at
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɔːl ɑːɹ nˌɑːt θˈiːvz ðæt dˈɑːɡz bˈɑːɹk æt

senses.definition: used to imply that people should not be judged solely based on accusations or appearances, and that it is important to assess the facts and evidence before making any conclusions

examples.x: Many people assume that all politicians are corrupt, but it's important to remember that all are not thieves that dogs bark at, and there are many honest and dedicated public servants working hard to make a positive impact.

word_list.pronunciation: ðə bˈeɪt hˈaɪdz kˈʌvɚz ðə hˈʊk

senses.definition: used to warn that people or situations may be deliberately deceptive, and that it is important to be cautious and discerning in order to avoid being taken advantage of or harmed

examples.x: The company's marketing strategy was to offer a free trial, but the bait hides the hook as the real costs were hidden in the fine print.

word_list.pronunciation: bˈɪɾɚ pˈɪlz mˈeɪ mˌaɪt kæn hæv blˈɛst swˈiːt hˈoʊlsʌm ɪfˈɛkts

senses.definition: used to imply  that sometimes unpleasant experiences or difficult decisions may have positive or beneficial outcomes over time
used to suggest that people should not judge others based solely on their outward demeanor or behavior, as someone who appears cold or distant may actually be warm and caring

word_list.word: everyone's faults are not written (in|on) their [forehead]
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹɪwˌʌnz fˈɑːlts ɑːɹ nˌɑːt ɹˈɪʔn̩ ɪn ðɛɹ fˈɔːɹhɛd

senses.definition: used to imply that people should not be judged or criticized based solely on their external appearance or behavior, as underlying factors may not be immediately apparent

examples.x: I try to give people the benefit of the doubt and assume the best in them, as everyone's faults are not written in their forehead.

word_list.word: {not} think there are no crocodiles (just|) because the water is calm
word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt θˈɪŋk ðɛɹˌɑːɹ nˈoʊ kɹˈɑːkədˌaɪlz dʒˈʌst bɪkˈʌz ðə wˈɔːɾɚɹ ɪz kˈɑːm

senses.definition: used to emphasize that even in seemingly safe situations, people should be aware of potential dangers and threats

word_list.word: the lion is not so fierce, as he (too|) is painted
word_list.pronunciation: ðə lˈaɪən ɪz nˌɑːt sˌoʊ fˈɪɹs æz hiː tˈuː ɪz pˈeɪntᵻd

senses.definition: use to imply that appearances or reputations can be deceiving and should not be relied upon to judge someone or something's true character or abilities

examples.x: The athlete was intimidated by his opponent's reputation, but soon discovered that the lion is not so fierce as he is painted.
